question_title: Will the interest in “elissa slotkin” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-11 according to Google Trends?
summary: The Mar 30 value in the resolution date range (2026-03-12 to 2026-04-11) is 37, which is itself an elevated post-spike value from Slotkin's viral Bill Maher appearance on Mar 28-29 (peak = 100). Historical patterns show Google Trends interest for this senator returns to baseline (8-15) within 3-5 days after any spike. By Apr 11 (12 days out), interest will almost certainly be at baseline, well below the 34 threshold for "Decreases." The narrow [34-40] window for "Doesn't change" has zero historical precedent in 50 days of data. "Increases" (value > 40) would require another major spike landing on or very near Apr 11 - possible given Slotkin's active political profile but unlikely for any specific day. Monte Carlo simulations consistently yield ~80-85% for Decreases.

factors.description: Post-spike decay: Mar 28-29 spike (100) driven by Bill Maher appearance already dropped to 37 by Mar 30. Historical decay patterns show return to baseline (8-15) within 3-5 days. By Apr 11 (12 days out), interest almost certainly at baseline.

Baseline interest is very low: In the resolution date range with peak=100, typical non-news days show values of 8-15. 90% of days in the 3-month history are below 34. The threshold for Decreases (value < 34) encompasses the vast majority of normal days.

Slotkin is in an active political period with multiple ongoing stories (prediction markets bill, AI Guardrails Act, Iran debate, SAVE Act, DHS funding), giving slightly elevated probability of another spike near Apr 11.

Narrow Doesn't Change window: Requires Apr 11 value in [34, 40] - a 7-point range. Zero days in 50-day history fell in this exact range. Only achievable if a moderate spike happens to land very close to Apr 11.
SerpAPI measurement uncertainty: Values can differ by ±2 from pytrends, adding noise but insufficient to close the ~26-point gap between current value (37) and expected baseline (~11).

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (83%)**

Key reasoning:

- **Starting point is post-spike elevated (37)**: The Bill Maher appearance on Mar 28 drove a massive spike to 100. By Mar 30 it's already fallen to 37 in the resolution range.
- **Historical decay**: Every prior spike for Slotkin returned to baseline (8-15) within 3-5 days. With 12 days until Apr 11, the decay will be complete.
- **90% of days are below 34**: The empirical distribution strongly favors Decreases territory. Only ~10% of days hit the elevated levels needed for Increases (>40).
- **"Doesn't change" is near-impossible**: The [34-40] window has zero hits in 50 days of history — it's a narrow band that requires a perfectly-sized spike on the exact day.
- **Active but not sustained**: While Slotkin is involved in multiple legislative efforts, her interest pattern is spiky (not sustained), making a specifically Apr 11 spike unlikely.

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| Decreases | **83%** |
| Increases | 12% |
| Doesn't change | 5% |
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “elissa slotkin” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-11 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42988)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=elissa%20slotkin&date=2026-03-12%202026-04-11](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=elissa%20slotkin&date=2026-03-12%202026-04-11) for 2026-04-11 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-30. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "elissa slotkin",
  "date": "2026-03-12 2026-04-11",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
